title: A New Way to Treat Depression: Could Immune System Drugs Help?
subtitle: Early research suggests that medicine used for arthritis might offer hope for patients who do not respond to standard treatments.

summary.en: Researchers are testing if an immune system drug called tocilizumab can help people with severe depression. Many patients do not get better with current antidepressants that target brain chemicals. A small study found that patients receiving this immunotherapy showed improvements in their symptoms. Experts believe this could lead to more personalized medical care in the future. While the results are promising, scientists note that more research is needed to confirm these findings.
summary.zh: 研究人員正在測試一種名為托珠單抗的免疫系統藥物，是否能幫助重度憂鬱症患者。許多患者在使用針對大腦化學物質的現有抗憂鬱藥物後並未好轉。一項小型研究發現，接受這種免疫療法的患者症狀有所改善。專家認為，這可能在未來帶來更個人化的醫療照護。儘管結果令人期待，但科學家指出，仍需更多研究來證實這些發現。

paragraphs.en: For millions of people around the world, depression is a difficult and long-lasting challenge. While many treatments exist, they do not work for everyone. According to The Guardian Health, about one in three people with depression do not get better using common medicines. These standard treatments usually focus on changing chemicals in the brain. However, a new study suggests that a different approach—one that focuses on the body’s immune system—might be a key to helping those who have not found relief elsewhere.
paragraphs.zh: 對於全球數百萬人來說，憂鬱症是一項艱難且持久的挑戰。雖然目前存在許多治療方法，但並非對每個人都有效。根據《衛報健康》（The Guardian Health）報導，約有三分之一的憂鬱症患者在使用常見藥物後病情並未好轉。這些標準治療通常著重於改變大腦中的化學物質。然而，一項新研究顯示，一種不同的方法——即針對人體免疫系統——或許是幫助那些在其他治療中找不到緩解方案的患者之關鍵。

paragraphs.en: Researchers at the University of Bristol recently tested a drug called tocilizumab. This medicine is a type of immunotherapy, which is a treatment that helps the immune system work differently. Tocilizumab is already used to treat immune conditions like rheumatoid arthritis, a disease that causes painful swelling in the joints. The drug works by blocking a specific receptor in the body called IL-6R. By blocking this receptor, the drug stops certain signals that cause inflammation, or swelling, in the body.
paragraphs.zh: 布里斯托大學（University of Bristol）的研究人員近期測試了一種名為托珠單抗（tocilizumab）的藥物。這種藥物屬於免疫療法，是一種協助免疫系統以不同方式運作的治療手段。托珠單抗目前已被用於治療類風濕性關節炎等免疫疾病，這是一種會導致關節疼痛腫脹的疾病。該藥物的作用機制是阻斷體內一種稱為 IL-6R 的特定受體。透過阻斷此受體，藥物能停止體內導致發炎或腫脹的特定訊號。

paragraphs.en: In a small clinical trial, researchers looked at 30 people who had moderate to severe depression. These participants had previously tried standard antidepressants without success. During the four-week study, half of the group received tocilizumab, while the other half received a placebo—a substance that looks like medicine but has no active ingredients. MedPage Today reports that the patients who received the drug showed greater improvements in their depression, fatigue, and anxiety compared to those who received the placebo.
paragraphs.zh: 在一項小型臨床試驗中，研究人員觀察了 30 名患有中度至重度憂鬱症的受試者。這些參與者先前曾嘗試過標準抗憂鬱藥物，但皆未見成效。在這項為期四週的研究中，半數受試者接受了托珠單抗，另一半則接受安慰劑——即外觀與藥物相同但無活性成分的物質。《MedPage Today》報導指出，與接受安慰劑的患者相比，接受該藥物的患者在憂鬱、疲勞及焦慮症狀上皆有顯著改善。

paragraphs.en: Golam Khandakar, a professor at the University of Bristol and the senior author of the study, described the trial as an important milestone. He noted that this is one of the first times researchers have used this specific approach to target depression. The results showed that 54% of the patients who received the drug reached a state of remission—a period where symptoms are gone or greatly reduced—compared to 31% in the placebo group. The researchers explained that this suggests the drug could be more effective than some common first-line antidepressants.
paragraphs.zh: 布里斯托大學教授兼該研究資深作者坎達卡（Golam Khandakar）將此試驗描述為一個重要的里程碑。他指出，這是研究人員首次使用這種特定方法來針對憂鬱症進行治療。結果顯示，接受該藥物的患者中有 54% 達到了緩解狀態（即症狀消失或大幅減輕的時期），而安慰劑組則為 31%。研究人員解釋，這顯示該藥物可能比某些常見的一線抗憂鬱藥物更為有效。

paragraphs.en: Dr. Éimear Foley, a co-author of the study, explained why this research is so important. She noted that depression affects between 10% and 20% of people during their lifetime. Because every person’s biology is different, current treatments do not always fit every patient. Dr. Foley stated that this study moves medicine closer to "tailored care," where doctors can choose treatments that match a person's specific biological needs. This could help doctors provide the right treatment to the right person at the right time.
paragraphs.zh: 該研究的共同作者佛利博士（Dr. Éimear Foley）解釋了這項研究為何如此重要。她指出，憂鬱症影響著 10% 到 20% 的人一生中的健康。由於每個人的生理構造不同，目前的治療方法並不總是適合每一位患者。佛利博士表示，這項研究讓醫學界更接近「量身打造的照護」，讓醫師能選擇符合個人特定生理需求的治療方式。這有助於醫師在正確的時間，為正確的對象提供正確的治療。

paragraphs.en: While these early results are exciting, the researchers were careful to explain that the study involved a small number of people. Because the group was small, it is hard to say for sure that the drug is the only reason for the improvement. However, the study provides early evidence that targeting inflammation could be a successful way to treat depression in the future. The findings were also discussed in the context of broader mental health trends, as MedPage Today noted that reports of anxiety and depression have been rising in various groups, including in the United States.
paragraphs.zh: 雖然這些初步結果令人振奮，但研究人員謹慎地說明，該研究的參與人數較少。由於樣本數有限，很難斷定藥物是導致改善的唯一原因。不過，這項研究提供了初步證據，顯示針對發炎反應可能是未來治療憂鬱症的成功途徑。這些發現也在更廣泛的心理健康趨勢背景下進行了討論，正如《MedPage Today》所指出的，包括美國在內的多個群體中，焦慮與憂鬱的通報案例皆呈現上升趨勢。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, this research opens the door for larger studies. If future trials confirm that immunotherapy can help, it could change how doctors treat patients who suffer from the most difficult forms of depression. By moving beyond just brain chemicals and looking at the immune system, scientists hope to offer new hope to those who have struggled to find effective treatment for years. As the medical community continues to study these connections, patients and doctors alike will be watching closely to see if this approach can become a standard part of mental health care.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，這項研究為更大規模的試驗開啟了大門。若未來的試驗證實免疫療法確實有效，將可能改變醫師治療最難治型憂鬱症患者的方式。透過跳脫僅關注大腦化學物質的框架，轉而研究免疫系統，科學家希望能為那些多年來難以找到有效治療方法的患者帶來新希望。隨著醫學界持續研究這些關聯，病患與醫師都將密切關注，看這種方法是否能成為心理健康照護的標準流程。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop or prevent something from moving or functioning.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 阻擋；阻止（某事發生或運作）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（街區），這裡作動詞用，指藥物抑制受體功能。文中：The drug works by blocking a specific receptor in the body called IL-6R.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be suitable or appropriate for a specific person or situation.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞（健康的）或名詞（發作），這裡作動詞，指治療方式是否適用於患者。文中：Because every person’s biology is different, current treatments do not always fit every patient.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To create an opportunity for something to happen in the future.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 開啟……的可能性；為……創造機會。
keyTerms.noteZh: 非字面意思，指研究結果為後續的大型實驗創造了條件。文中：Looking ahead, this research opens the door for larger studies.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Immunotherapy could be used to treat depression, early trial suggests

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: IL-6 Drug Promising in Depression; U.S. More Anxious; AI Risks in Addiction Medicine

quiz.question: According to the study results mentioned in the article, what percentage of patients who received tocilizumab achieved a state of remission?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第四段，研究結果顯示接受 tocilizumab 藥物治療的患者中，有 54% 達到了緩解狀態（remission）。選項 B（31%）是安慰劑組的數據，而非藥物組；選項 A 和 D 則是文中提到的憂鬱症盛行率範圍，與藥物療效數據無關。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, what can be inferred about the future of depression treatment?

options.A: Immunotherapy will soon replace all existing antidepressant medications.
options.B: Doctors will likely shift toward personalized treatment plans based on a patient's biological profile.
options.C: Inflammation is the primary cause of depression in all patients worldwide.
options.D: The current study is definitive proof that tocilizumab is the best cure for anxiety.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第五段，Dr. Foley 提到這項研究使醫學更接近「量身打造的照護」（tailored care），即根據患者的生物需求選擇治療方式。選項 A 太過武斷，文中僅說可能成為一種選擇；選項 C 錯誤，文中並未稱發炎是唯一成因；選項 D 錯誤，研究人員強調樣本數小，尚需更多研究證實。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the phrase 'tailored care' mean in the context of the article?

options.A: Treatments that are mass-produced to be cheaper for everyone.
options.B: Medical approaches customized to meet an individual's specific biological needs.
options.C: A method of stitching together different types of traditional therapies.
options.D: Care that focuses exclusively on the patient's mental health history.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中提到「tailored care」是為了回應每個人生物特徵（biology）的不同，因此醫生需選擇適合該患者特定需求的治療。選項 A 與「量身打造」的意義相反；選項 C 是對「tailored」一詞的字面誤解（裁縫）；選項 D 範圍過窄，忽略了文中強調的生物學基礎。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: Standard antidepressants are dangerous and should be avoided by all patients.
options.B: The University of Bristol has discovered a permanent cure for all types of depression.
options.C: New research suggests that targeting the immune system could be a promising alternative for treating depression.
options.D: Rheumatoid arthritis is the leading cause of depression in modern society.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文的核心在於介紹一項關於免疫療法（tocilizumab）治療憂鬱症的初步研究，並探討其作為新治療途徑的潛力。選項 A 文中未提及藥物危險性；選項 B 過度誇大研究成果；選項 D 錯誤，文中僅提到該藥物原用於治療類風濕性關節炎，並非指該病是憂鬱症的主因。
